wormhole_accum._role: TRANSIENT POOLED scatter accumulation (recomputed each frame): rgb = additively-accumulated scatter deposits (src.rgb * lum * lum), a unused. Full-res. Cleared (clear), scattered into additively via drawMode:'points' Blend One One (deposit), then mean-normalized/sqrt'd and blended with the input (blend). NOT 'global_'-prefixed, NOT a state surface, NOT double-buffered/persisted — a regular pooled graph texture per reference 04 §1 (liveness/pooling). Format rgba16f per definition.js (NOT a state texture, so NOT rgba32f).

feedback._comment: Multi-pass POINTS-SCATTER filter with NO persistent agent state and NO frame-to-frame feedback. 3 passes/frame in order: clear (fullscreen) -> deposit (drawMode:'points', additive) -> blend (fullscreen). Unlike the agent-based pattern there are no 'global_' state textures (no xyz/vel/rgba/trail) and no isStateSurface persistence; the single intermediate wormhole_accum is a TRANSIENT pooled graph texture (rgba16f, full-res) rebuilt every frame. The deposit pass uses drawMode:'points' with count:'input' → the runtime resolves N = inputTex.width * inputTex.height (ONE DrawProcedural Points vertex per INPUT pixel — NOT stateSize*stateSize; there are no agents). The deposit VERTEX stage reads inputTex via SV_VertexID (vert_deposit), extracts OKLab L, computes the scatter destination texel (with mirror/repeat/clamp wrap), maps it to clip space (WGSL top-left mapping: clipY = 1 - (destY+0.5)/h*2) and emits a 1px point; the fragment writes src.rgb*lum*lum (a=0) and the pass blends additively (Blend One One). No MRT (single fragColor per pass). No repeat. No '_iteration' uniform.
